_note: Full-coverage fixture for hooks/session-start/detect-repo-instructions.sh, built in Phase 0 iteration 0.4 on top of 0.2's single proving case (surfaces-root-agents-md, kept unchanged below). Every case's expected output was captured by running the unmodified bash hook directly against a staged throwaway CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR before being written here — never assumed. Three behaviors worth flagging for the Node port (see the per-case notes below for detail): (1) the root-file loop's all 4 filenames each have a dedicated case now (CLAUDE.md via surfaces-root-claude-md, AGENTS.md via surfaces-root-agents-md, .cursorrules and copilot-instructions.md via their own cases) — added in pass 2 (F-15) after review caught that CLAUDE.md, the hook's own namesake file, was reachable only through the nested find scan in every other case and so a port dropping it from the root array would have passed all 8 prior cases undetected; (2) the nested-file find's maxdepth/mindepth boundary is inclusive at 2 nested subdirectories and exclusive at 3 — verified by direct find invocation, not inferred from the flag values; (3) the hook's documented prune list (.git/node_modules/.somi/vendor) is currently NOT effective for files one level inside those directories, because GNU find's -mindepth 2 suppresses ALL predicate evaluation (including the -prune test) for entries at depth < 2 — the prune group can therefore never fire, since the pruned directories themselves are always depth-1 children of CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR. This is surprising, current, reproducible bash behavior (confirmed directly with /usr/bin/find, GNU findutils 4.9.0, independent of any shell find() wrapper, and independently reproduced by review with a control experiment: the identical find expression without -mindepth 2 prunes correctly) that a naive Node port would very plausibly 'fix' (i.e. implement working exclusion), which would be an undetected behavior change unless a fixture pins the current (broken) exclusion. Preserved here per D2's behavior-preservation mandate; a deliberate fix, if wanted, is a decision for Phase 2 iteration 2.7 to raise explicitly, not something to silently correct during the port. Scope note: this corpus, especially the prune-list case, is captured against GNU findutils (confirmed 4.9.0, Linux); BSD/macOS find is documented to treat -mindepth/-prune interaction differently, so 2.7's parity decision on the prune behavior is explicitly GNU-scoped, not a cross-platform finding.

cases.name: surfaces-root-claude-md
cases._note: F-15 fix (0.4 pass 2): the namesake root-file-loop entry. Every other CLAUDE.md-bearing case in this fixture (a/b/CLAUDE.md, a/b/c/CLAUDE.md, node_modules/CLAUDE.md) reaches the file through the nested find scan, never through the root -f loop at detect-repo-instructions.sh:30 -- so a port whose root-file array drops CLAUDE.md passed all 8 prior cases undetected. This case closes that gap directly.

cases.name: no-instructions-silent-exit
cases._note: No repo-local instruction files and no .claude/agents/ present anywhere in the staged (empty) project dir -> the hook's FOUND array and REPO_AGENTS are both empty, so it exits 0 with no hookSpecificOutput at all.

cases.name: surfaces-root-cursorrules
cases._note: Pins a second entry in the root-file loop distinct from AGENTS.md, so a port that drops .cursorrules from its filename list is caught.

cases.name: surfaces-root-copilot-instructions
cases._note: Third entry in the root-file loop; this repo itself will ship this exact file per D3 (Phase 4), so this branch matters in practice, not just in theory.

cases.name: surfaces-nested-claude-md-at-max-scan-depth
cases._note: Nested find is `find PROJECT_ROOT -maxdepth 3 ... -mindepth 2 -type f`. PROJECT_ROOT itself is depth 0, so a file two directories down (a/b/CLAUDE.md) is depth 3 -- the deepest depth the scan reaches. Verified directly: this file IS surfaced.

cases.name: omits-claude-md-beyond-scan-depth
cases._note: Companion to the case above, one directory deeper (a/b/c/CLAUDE.md, depth 4) -- exceeds -maxdepth 3, so find never visits it. Verified directly: this file is NOT surfaced and the hook exits 0 with no context (no other FOUND entries or REPO_AGENTS in this staged dir).

cases.name: surfaces-claude-agents-dir-with-count
cases._note: Pins the exact count-message format: '<count> repo-local subagent definition(s))' with the staged entry count interpolated (2 .md files staged here).

cases.name: surfaces-nested-claude-md-under-pruned-dir
cases._note: Pins the current (surprising) bash behavior described in the file-level _note above: node_modules is in the hook's documented prune list, but a CLAUDE.md one level inside it (depth 2) IS surfaced under current bash, because -mindepth 2 suppresses the -prune test at node_modules' own depth (1), so find never actually skips descending into it. This is NOT an endorsement of the behavior -- it is what the current hook does, and the fixture's job is to freeze that for the port, not to silently correct it.

files.node_modules/CLAUDE.md: # should be pruned but currently is not
